requirements: Composer require any package-level composerRequires before seeding demo data.
Run package setup or demo commands listed in the package overview.
Authenticate as an admin user with the required role or permission.
Resolve admin-surface targets through Filament resource/page URLs when possible.
Resolve frontend-url targets through seeded demo routes or package route names.
Execute package-level browserTests when declared by the source screenshots manifest.

entries.title: Access areas admin index.
entries.notes: Requires package migrations plus at least one seeded access area with a known route key, status, registration policy, and token policy.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews which protected areas are active and how each area accepts or blocks access requests.

entries.title: Access area create/edit form.
entries.notes: Capture the key, status, schedule, registration policy, token policy, approval limit, allowlist, and metadata fields. The batch harness still needs install-flow verification for access-gate migrations.
entries.useCase: An administrator configures a protected area before routing private content through the access-gate middleware.

entries.title: Registrations admin index with review actions.
entries.notes: Requires seeded pending, approved, rejected, and expired registrations tied to an access area so table filters and row actions are visible.
entries.useCase: An administrator triages access requests and uses approve, reject, resend claim, expire, or grant actions.

entries.title: Grants admin index.
entries.notes: Requires seeded active, expired, and revoked grants across at least one subject type.
entries.useCase: An administrator audits who currently has access and revokes grants that should no longer unlock protected content.

entries.title: Claim tokens admin index.
entries.notes: Requires seeded pending, consumed, and expired claim tokens linked to registrations or grants.
entries.useCase: An administrator checks whether approval links were issued, claimed, or expired.

entries.title: Browser tokens admin index.
entries.notes: Requires seeded active, expired, and revoked browser tokens tied to grants.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews browser-bound access and revokes a token after device loss or policy change.

entries.title: Access events admin index.
entries.notes: Requires seeded request, approval, rejection, claim, grant, revoke, and middleware-denied events.
entries.useCase: An administrator investigates the history of an access request or failed protected-page visit.

entries.title: Public access request form.
entries.notes: Capture anonymously against a seeded area that allows public registration. Verify the HTML contains no authoring markers, editor URLs, model IDs, or admin-only labels.
entries.useCase: A visitor requests access to a protected area by submitting the configured public registration fields.

entries.title: Public gated message.
entries.notes: Requires a seeded protected route using `access-gate:{area}` middleware and no active grant for the current visitor.
entries.useCase: A visitor without an active grant sees the blocked state instead of the protected content.

entries.title: Public request CTA component.
entries.notes: Capture direct access-gate submission in the base package. Capture the Public Actions variant only in a separate run with `capell-app/public-actions` installed.
entries.useCase: A visitor follows an inline request CTA from a protected page or teaser block.

entries.title: Countries admin index.
entries.notes: Requires package migrations and seeded country rows with ISO2/ISO3 codes and language relationships.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews available countries before using country and address selectors elsewhere in Capell.

entries.title: Addresses admin index.
entries.notes: Requires seeded addresses connected to seeded countries. Include at least one address with full line, city, state, postal code, and country data.
entries.useCase: An administrator manages reusable postal addresses that other packages can reference instead of duplicating location fields.

entries.title: Create/edit country form.
entries.notes: Open the create or edit modal from `CountryResource`; the current resource uses table actions rather than a separate create page.
entries.useCase: An administrator adds or corrects a country name, ISO code, and language mapping.

entries.title: Create/edit address form.
entries.notes: Open the create or edit modal from `AddressResource`; seed countries first so the country selector is meaningful.
entries.useCase: An administrator creates a structured address with country, street, locality, region, and postal fields.

entries.title: Site settings fields where address data is injected.
entries.notes: Requires the host admin site form that consumes `SiteSchemaExtender` plus seeded countries and addresses.
entries.useCase: A site administrator selects reusable address data while editing site-level settings.

entries.title: Agent Bridge prompt builder page.
entries.notes: Requires `capell-app/agent-bridge` installed, settings migrated, and an admin session. This page is available at the Filament slug `capell-agent-bridge/prompt-builder`.
entries.useCase: An administrator builds a scoped prompt for an external agent using the installed Capell package context.

entries.title: Token management or setup surface.
entries.notes: The token UI is exposed through user-resource schema/relation manager integration, not a standalone resource. Capture from a host user edit screen after seeding an agent bridge token.
entries.useCase: An administrator creates or reviews a token used by a trusted Agent Bridge client.

entries.title: Capability preview and confirmation flow.
entries.notes: Requires a seeded capability provider and a pending `CapellAgentBridgeConfirmation`; the base page alone will not show a confirmation flow.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews a proposed capability run before confirming that an agent may execute it.

entries.title: Audit entry review.
entries.notes: Capture from the user-resource audit relation manager after seeding capability invocation records.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews what an Agent Bridge client previewed, confirmed, or ran for a user.

entries.title: Agent Bridge server health output.
entries.notes: Requires enabled Agent Bridge routes in `capell-agent-bridge.php`. Capture route/config health from the prompt builder or a harness health panel if the host app exposes one.
entries.useCase: An operator confirms which Agent Bridge endpoints are enabled and whether token-authenticated requests can reach them.

entries.title: Capability list or prompt surface where provided by a consuming package.
entries.notes: Optional because this package registers services and actions but no standalone Filament UI. Capture only through a consuming package that renders registered `AIOrchestratorCapabilityData` entries.
entries.useCase: A developer verifies that installed AI Orchestrator modules expose the expected capability labels and approval levels.

entries.title: LayoutBuilder layout preview workflow if LayoutBuilder integration is enabled.
entries.notes: Optional and blocked until a Layout Builder consuming UI exposes `LayoutBuilderAIOrchestratorModule`; this package only provides the integration module and preview action.
entries.useCase: An editor previews an AI-generated Layout Builder plan before any layout content is applied.

entries.title: Approval state where a capability requires review.
entries.notes: Optional and requires a consuming package that persists or displays review-required capability runs. AI Orchestrator itself keeps run state in data objects.
entries.useCase: A reviewer sees that an AI capability result needs human approval before the consuming package acts on it.

entries.title: Successful page resolve JSON response.
entries.notes: Requires API installed, Layout Builder installed, and a seeded published page with a resolvable URL. Capture the JSON body plus `X-Capell-Api-Version` and `X-Capell-Cache-Tags` headers.
entries.useCase: A frontend renderer resolves a published Capell page into public JSON for rendering outside the CMS.

entries.title: Page resolve not-found JSON response.
entries.notes: Use a missing URL on an installed package and verify the response remains generic, with no internal model names, editor markers, or draft content.
entries.useCase: An API client receives a predictable not-found response for an unknown public page URL.

entries.title: Forbidden explicit context response.
entries.notes: Request an explicit site or language without a valid context signature and capture the forbidden JSON response.
entries.useCase: An API consumer learns that explicit site/language context requires a valid signature.

entries.title: Page resolve layout graph JSON response.
entries.notes: Requires Layout Builder and a seeded page with bounded layout content. Verify public JSON does not expose authoring metadata, field paths, or signed editor URLs.
entries.useCase: A frontend renderer requests the bounded layout graph for a published page.

entries.title: Block registry diagnostics output.
entries.notes: Optional because `capell-app/content-blocks` is a shared registry package. Requires a consuming package or harness that registers block definitions before diagnostics are meaningful.
entries.useCase: A package developer verifies which block definitions, renderers, fixtures, and builder blocks are registered.

entries.title: Filament builder block picker in a consuming package.
entries.notes: Optional and blocked until a consuming package such as Content Sections exposes registered `FilamentBuilderBlock` classes in a Builder field.
entries.useCase: An editor chooses a registered content block from a Filament Builder field supplied by a consuming package.

entries.title: Fallback block rendering state.
entries.notes: Capture only in a controlled fixture. Public output must not reveal internal block metadata, admin view names, or package implementation details.
entries.useCase: A developer confirms that an unknown or unrenderable block fails closed with safe public fallback output.

entries.title: Articles admin index.
entries.notes: Requires Blog plus its required Capell extensions installed, package migrations, and seeded draft/published articles with sites, layouts, URLs, translations, and tags.
entries.useCase: An editor reviews the article queue, publish windows, language coverage, and article status before editing content.

entries.title: Create/edit article form.
entries.notes: Requires seeded sites, article type, layouts, and tags so relationship fields are populated. The batch harness could not complete extension install until required dependency extensions were marked installed.
entries.useCase: An editor creates or updates an article with publishing windows, metadata, layout selection, media, and taxonomy.

entries.title: Article health dashboard widget.
entries.notes: Capture when the host dashboard registers the Blog widget implementation and seeded articles include complete/incomplete metadata states.
entries.useCase: An editor sees whether articles are missing metadata, tags, media, translations, or publish settings.

entries.title: Top pages dashboard widget.
entries.notes: Requires the host dashboard to register the widget and seeded article/page traffic data from the analytics package used by the host.
entries.useCase: An editor identifies which articles or blog pages are drawing the most visits.

entries.title: Traffic chart dashboard widget.
entries.notes: Requires the host dashboard to register the widget and seeded time-series traffic data.
entries.useCase: An editor reviews recent traffic trends for blog content from the dashboard.

entries.title: Blog page frontend output.
entries.notes: Requires seeded blog landing page, published articles, layout content, URLs, and navigation. Verify public output contains no authoring markers or admin/editor URLs.
entries.useCase: A visitor browses the public blog landing page and sees published article listings.

entries.title: Archive page frontend output.
entries.notes: Requires archive pages created by setup/demo commands and articles with visible dates across at least two months.
entries.useCase: A visitor opens a monthly archive and sees articles filtered to that archive period.

entries.title: Tag page frontend output.
entries.notes: Requires seeded tags attached to published articles and tag page URLs from the blog setup flow.
entries.useCase: A visitor opens a tag page and sees published articles associated with that tag.

entries.title: Article page block output.
entries.notes: Requires a seeded article page with author, date, tag, media, and related metadata. Capture as anonymous public output.
entries.useCase: A visitor reads a public article block with the article metadata needed for scanning and navigation.

entries.title: Article tags block output.
entries.notes: Requires seeded tags with at least one published article per tag. Capture as anonymous public output.
entries.useCase: A visitor uses the tag block to navigate between related article topics.

entries.title: Campaign groups index.
entries.notes: Requires Campaign Studio plus required Capell extensions installed, package migrations, and seeded campaign groups linked to sites.
entries.useCase: A marketer reviews active campaign groups before managing landing pages, CTAs, and goals.

entries.title: Campaign landing pages index.
entries.notes: Requires seeded campaign groups, target pages, primary goals, and campaign URLs. The batch harness still needs dependency extension state fixed before final install screenshots.
entries.useCase: A marketer checks which public pages are attached to campaigns and which goal each page should optimize for.

entries.title: Campaign conversion goals form.
entries.notes: Requires at least one seeded campaign group. Include goal type, attribution model, and active/inactive state fields in the capture.
entries.useCase: A marketer defines the measurable outcome that campaign landing pages and conversions report against.

entries.title: CTA block form.
entries.notes: Requires a seeded campaign group and target URL or target page. Capture title, body, button/action, style, and tracking fields.
entries.useCase: A marketer creates a reusable campaign CTA block with conversion tracking metadata.

entries.title: Campaign dashboard blocks.
entries.notes: Requires the host dashboard to register Campaign Studio widgets and seeded visits, events, landing pages, goals, and conversions.
entries.useCase: A marketer reviews campaign performance, top campaign groups, and top landing pages from the admin dashboard.

entries.title: Frontend landing page with campaign blocks.
entries.notes: Requires a seeded public page using campaign hero, CTA, or lead-form blocks plus Form Builder and Layout Builder. Verify public HTML does not expose admin-only campaign metadata.
entries.useCase: A visitor lands on a campaign page with tracked hero, CTA, and lead-capture content.

entries.title: Reusable sections index.
entries.notes: Capture after installing content-sections with content-blocks and layout-builder, with at least three seeded sections across different blueprints so table filters, columns, and navigation state are visible.
entries.useCase: An editor reviews reusable sections before choosing which shared content block to update.

entries.title: Create reusable section form.
entries.notes: Capture with the blueprint selector visible. The form depends on Layout Builder blueprints registered by the installed package.
entries.useCase: An editor starts a new reusable section and chooses the section blueprint that controls the available fields.

entries.title: Edit reusable section with assets.
entries.notes: Requires a seeded section with translations and at least one related block asset so the relation manager tab is not empty.
entries.useCase: An editor updates shared section copy and checks which block assets are attached before saving.

entries.title: Section selector modal.
entries.notes: Requires a host admin workflow that opens ModalTableSelect, such as attaching section assets from a block or layout field.
entries.useCase: An editor searches existing reusable sections from the modal before attaching one to another content surface.

entries.title: Frontend section block gallery.
entries.notes: Seed one public page that renders each registered section block family. Anonymous output must not expose authoring markers, editor URLs, internal IDs, or package internals.
entries.useCase: A maintainer verifies the public section gallery renders every package-owned block safely for anonymous visitors.

entries.title: Publishing trend dashboard widget.
entries.notes: Capture on the main admin dashboard with seeded published and scheduled page counts across the selected date window.
entries.useCase: An administrator checks whether publishing and scheduling activity changed during the current reporting window.

entries.title: Content health dashboard widget.
entries.notes: Capture on the main admin dashboard with at least one scheduled, expired, URL-less, or stale page so canView() keeps the widget visible.
entries.useCase: An editor spots page health issues that need attention before the next publishing review.

entries.title: Dashboard report visibility settings.
entries.notes: Capture the shared dashboard settings screen after this package contributes the publishing trend and content health visibility keys.
entries.useCase: A site owner confirms the Dashboard Reports widgets can be enabled or hidden through dashboard settings.

entries.title: Demo Kit admin page.
entries.notes: Capture as an admin who can manage extensions, with the Insert example site data header action available. Opening the action modal is useful when the runner supports modal state.
entries.useCase: An administrator starts deterministic demo content generation from the package-owned admin page.

entries.title: Generated demo page content block.
entries.notes: Requires Demo Kit generated page data with a layout block using the demo-page-content renderable. Public output should show package Blade presentation, not stored Tailwind markup from database content.
entries.useCase: A maintainer verifies generated demo pages render designed content through the package-owned Blade view.

entries.title: Generated homepage section block.
entries.notes: Requires a generated homepage layout that includes a homepage-section renderable such as the command-centre hero block.
entries.useCase: A maintainer checks the generated homepage uses portable CMS data with presentation kept in package Blade.

entries.title: Deployment connection page.
entries.notes: Capture as a user with View:DeploymentConnectionPage. The empty state only shows provider OAuth buttons; seed one active connection when the screenshot should include repository details.
entries.useCase: An operator connects or reviews the Git provider repository used for package install pull requests.

entries.title: Deployment connection widget.
entries.notes: Capture with one active DeploymentConnection when possible; otherwise the widget honestly shows the not-connected state.
entries.useCase: An administrator checks from the dashboard whether package deployment publishing has an active repository connection.

entries.title: Developer tools dashboard.
entries.notes: Capture as a super admin or a user with diagnostics access. The page depends on registered makers and registry inspector data from the installed host app.
entries.useCase: A developer inspects package maker safety, registered configurators, components, and blocks from the diagnostics dashboard.

entries.title: Health widgets on the admin dashboard.
entries.notes: Capture the main admin dashboard or System Health dashboard after diagnostics widgets register. Some widgets show richer output only when the host app has package, migration, cache, Tailwind, or content graph state to report.
entries.useCase: An operator checks the installed site's health widgets before investigating a deployment or publishing issue.

entries.title: Command palette page.
entries.notes: Capture as a super admin or diagnostics user. Select a safe navigation command if the runner can preserve selected-command state; avoid executing destructive capell:* commands during screenshot capture.
entries.useCase: A developer searches trusted diagnostics and capell:* commands, reviews required parameters, and sees confirmation state before execution.

entries.title: System health page.
entries.notes: Requires a super admin role and capell.dashboard.system_health_enabled=true. Widget detail depends on host setup, migration, package, registry, content graph, cache, config, and Tailwind state.
entries.useCase: A super admin reviews system health panels before changing package setup or publishing configuration.

entries.title: Permission audit page.
entries.notes: Requires a user with the viewPermissionAuditPage diagnostics permission and registered permissions/roles in the host app.
entries.useCase: An administrator audits registered permissions and role coverage after installing or removing packages.

entries.title: Queue health page.
entries.notes: Capture with at least one failed job row when possible; an empty queue table is valid but less useful for documentation.
entries.useCase: An operator reviews failed job summaries and queue health before retrying or debugging background work.

entries.title: Controlled documents index.
entries.notes: Capture with at least one active document and one draft or archived document so status badges and publication counts are visible.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews controlled documents and sees which policy or legal records have published versions.

entries.title: Controlled document edit form.
entries.notes: Requires a seeded Document record. The key field is disabled; title, status, metadata, and relation manager tabs should be visible.
entries.useCase: An administrator updates the title, lifecycle status, or metadata for a controlled document.

entries.title: Document publications relation manager.
entries.notes: Requires at least one DocumentPublication, ideally one created from a Publishing Studio revision so published_revision_id is populated.
entries.useCase: A compliance reviewer checks immutable publication versions, hashes, revision IDs, and publish timestamps for a document.

entries.title: Document acceptances relation manager.
entries.notes: Requires seeded DocumentAcceptance rows linked to the controlled document. Include at least one context such as registration or account_update.
entries.useCase: A compliance reviewer confirms which version a user or workflow accepted and when that acceptance was recorded.

entries.title: Events index.
entries.notes: Capture with at least three seeded events across public/private or scheduled states so event table columns and controls are meaningful.
entries.useCase: An editor reviews upcoming event records before opening one for schedule or registration changes.

entries.title: Create event form.
entries.notes: Capture after event publishing defaults are registered so blueprint and layout defaults are available on create.
entries.useCase: An editor creates an event with schedule, recurrence, visibility, publishing, and registration settings.

entries.title: Edit event form.
entries.notes: Requires a seeded event with recurrence and at least one generated occurrence so event state is not blank.
entries.useCase: An editor adjusts an existing recurring event and checks registration or publication state before saving.

entries.title: Event venues management.
entries.notes: Capture with at least one venue that has address fields and a map URL so the management table is populated.
entries.useCase: An administrator manages reusable venues for events and confirms address details are visible.

entries.title: Event occurrences management.
entries.notes: Requires generated occurrences from SyncEventOccurrencesAction, including one override or cancelled occurrence when possible.
entries.useCase: An editor reviews materialized event dates, statuses, venues, and registration counts.

entries.title: Event registrations management.
entries.notes: Requires native RSVP demo data with at least one pending, confirmed, or waitlisted registration.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews event attendee registrations, quantities, statuses, and related occurrences.

entries.title: Admin event calendar page.
entries.notes: Requires View:EventCalendarPage permission and seeded occurrences within the next 90 days so the header widget has visible dates.
entries.useCase: An editor opens the admin calendar to scan event occurrences across the next publishing window.

entries.title: Event calendar widget.
entries.notes: Capture from EventCalendarPage or any host dashboard that renders EventCalendarWidget, with occurrences in the next 90 days.
entries.useCase: An editor checks the calendar widget for upcoming event dates without leaving the current admin context.

entries.title: Frontend events listing page.
entries.notes: Requires a public listing page, site/language context, and public non-cancelled occurrences. Anonymous output must not expose authoring markers, editor URLs, internal IDs, or package internals.
entries.useCase: A visitor browses public upcoming events for the current site.

entries.title: Frontend events calendar page.
entries.notes: Requires a public calendar page and seeded public occurrences. Anonymous output must stay free of editor controls, model IDs, signed URLs, and package internals.
entries.useCase: A visitor scans public events in calendar form without seeing admin-only metadata.

entries.title: iCalendar feed route.
entries.notes: The actual route name is capell-events.calendar-feed. Verify text/calendar output for seeded public events; screenshot can be a browser text capture of the feed body.
entries.useCase: A visitor or calendar client subscribes to the site's public event feed.

entries.title: FormBuilder admin index.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/form-builder in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews form definitions and their publication/status state.

entries.title: Create/edit form schema screen.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/form-builder in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: An editor builds or adjusts a form schema, field types, validation, and notification settings.

entries.title: Submissions index.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/form-builder in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: An administrator triages unread, spam, archived, and normal submissions.

entries.title: Frontend form output.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/form-builder in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: A visitor completes a seeded public form rendered through the frontend component.

entries.title: Submission detail view.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/form-builder in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: An administrator reads a submission payload and reply context without exposing encrypted raw storage.

entries.title: Default theme settings screen.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/foundation-theme in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: A site owner adjusts Foundation Theme settings and reviews theme defaults.

entries.title: Frontend page using the default theme.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/foundation-theme in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: A visitor sees a seeded page rendered by Foundation Theme with its header, footer, tokens, and components.

entries.title: Generated Tailwind asset output review.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/foundation-theme in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: A developer verifies the generated Tailwind entrypoint after package/theme asset registration.

entries.title: Foundation header Layout Builder area.
entries.notes: Capture a public page with a seeded header-area block to prove Foundation Theme renders the registered Layout Builder header area.
entries.useCase: A maintainer verifies the registered Layout Builder header area renders through Foundation Theme.

entries.title: Frontend page with authoring enabled for admins.
entries.notes: Authenticated frontend capture. The runner logs in as an admin, opens the public Capell app homepage, waits for the beacon to decorate editable regions, and hovers the first editable region so the authoring control is visible.
entries.useCase: An authenticated admin opens a public page and sees post-load editable regions after the beacon succeeds.

entries.title: Capell app frontend authoring working on the public homepage.
entries.notes: Documentation proof that capell-app renders the frontend authoring package working: an authenticated admin beacon decorates the public homepage with editable regions and visible edit controls.
entries.useCase: An authenticated admin verifies inline authoring controls against a real Capell app homepage.

entries.title: Capell app frontend authoring working on mobile.
entries.notes: Mobile proof that the authoring overlay works against the Capell app frontend without adding public authoring metadata to cached HTML.
entries.useCase: An authenticated admin verifies the authoring overlay remains usable on a mobile viewport.

entries.title: Beacon network request.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/frontend-authoring in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: A developer verifies the beacon request returns authoring data only for authenticated admins.

entries.title: Frontend authoring enabled/disabled configuration proof.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/frontend-authoring in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: A site owner confirms authoring can be enabled or disabled without leaking controls to public users.

entries.title: Frontend Optimizer profile asset output.
entries.notes: Capture a public page whose theme calls @frontendOptimizerAssets(...) and verify the emitted CSS and JavaScript tags match the seeded render profile.
entries.useCase: A developer verifies a render profile emits the expected CSS and JavaScript assets.

entries.title: Frontend Optimizer critical CSS output.
entries.notes: Capture the generated critical CSS or manifest artifact for a seeded render profile after the optimizer has run.
entries.useCase: A developer reviews generated critical CSS or its manifest artifact for a seeded render profile.

entries.title: GA4 Reports dashboard page.
entries.notes: Capture the extension page with overview stats, traffic trend, top pages, and setup status widgets.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews GA4 overview stats, traffic trends, top pages, and setup status.

entries.title: GA4 Reports setup status widget.
entries.notes: Capture the not-configured and configured states if the screenshot runner can seed both.
entries.useCase: A site owner distinguishes not-configured and configured GA4 states before enabling sync.

entries.title: GA4 Reports settings.
entries.notes: Capture the settings section for property ID, credentials path, route slug, sync window, and enabled state.
entries.useCase: A site owner configures property ID, credentials path, route slug, sync window, and enabled state.

entries.title: Hero home block rendered on a public page.
entries.notes: Requires seeded layout content or the capell:hero-setup command plus a frontend theme that renders the hero block.
entries.useCase: A visitor sees the seeded hero block rendered through Layout Builder and the active frontend theme.

entries.title: Hero slide or related content variant.
entries.notes: Capture when demo data includes slide or related hero content; omit for the minimal single-hero fixture.
entries.useCase: A visitor sees the optional seeded slide or related-content hero variation when demo content includes it.

entries.title: HTML Cache maintenance cache page.
entries.notes: Capture the maintenance cache controls after at least one site exists.
entries.useCase: An administrator clears, warms, or regenerates cached HTML for a site.

entries.title: Cached model URLs resource index.
entries.notes: Capture after warming a public page so cache map rows exist.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews model-to-URL cache map rows after a public page has been warmed.

entries.title: HTML Cache dashboard widgets.
entries.notes: Capture cache overview, cache coverage URLs, and stale queue widgets.
entries.useCase: An administrator checks cache overview, coverage, and stale queue widgets.

entries.title: HTML Cache site health cache map.
entries.notes: Capture package diagnostics and public output safety checks on the core Site Health page.
entries.useCase: An operator reviews cache-map diagnostics and public-output safety checks in Site Health.

entries.title: Page table cache indicator.
entries.notes: Capture the core Pages resource with the cache table extension visible.
entries.useCase: An editor sees cache state indicators directly in the core Pages resource.

entries.title: Anonymous public cache hit.
entries.notes: Capture anonymous public output and verify no authoring markers, editor URLs, or session cookies are exposed.
entries.useCase: An anonymous visitor receives cached public HTML with no authoring markers, editor URLs, or session cookies.

entries.title: Static maintenance page output.
entries.notes: Capture generated maintenance HTML for a site/domain after using the maintenance cache page.
entries.useCase: An anonymous visitor sees the generated static maintenance page for a site/domain.

entries.title: Insights overview dashboard widgets.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/insights in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews analytics overview stats for seeded visits and events.

entries.title: Popular pages widget.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/insights in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: An administrator identifies high-traffic pages from seeded page-view data.

entries.title: Recent journeys widget.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/insights in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: An administrator follows recent visitor journeys across pages and events.

entries.title: Insights settings screen.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/insights in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: A site owner configures tracking, consent, retention, and beacon behavior.

entries.title: Frontend page with tracker active.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/insights in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: A visitor loads a public page where the tracker script is active and consent rules are respected.

entries.title: Blocks admin index.
entries.notes: Shows reusable blocks, component/search columns, filters, and table actions.
entries.useCase: An administrator scans reusable blocks by component, usage, status, and search/filter state.

entries.title: Create/edit block form with block assets.
entries.notes: Capture the component, display, settings, translations, and asset configuration tabs.
entries.useCase: An editor configures a reusable block, its display settings, translations, and block assets.

entries.title: Block layouts relation manager.
entries.notes: Shows which layouts currently use a reusable block.
entries.useCase: An editor checks which layouts use a reusable block before editing or deleting it.

entries.title: Layouts admin index with Layout Builder table extensions.
entries.notes: Capture package-specific layout table columns, filters, and actions.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews layout records with Layout Builder table extensions and actions.

entries.title: Page form content-first layout editor.
entries.notes: Requires a seeded page and layout with editable block assets.
entries.useCase: An editor manages page content through the content-first layout editor for seeded block assets.

entries.title: Page hero editor extension.
entries.notes: Shows hero-specific page schema fields added by the package.
entries.useCase: An editor configures the hero fields injected into the page schema.

entries.title: Public main content layout render.
entries.notes: Anonymous public page render with seeded blocks; must expose no admin/editor state.
entries.useCase: An anonymous visitor sees seeded layout blocks rendered as safe public HTML.

entries.title: Public named layout area render.
entries.notes: Anonymous render of a named layout area such as header; must expose no authoring metadata.
entries.useCase: An anonymous visitor sees a named layout area, such as a header, rendered without authoring metadata.

entries.title: Layout health dashboard widget.
entries.notes: Capture when the host admin dashboard enables this widget.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews layout health counts when the host dashboard enables the widget.

entries.title: Recent layout activity dashboard widget.
entries.notes: Capture when the host admin dashboard enables this widget.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews recent layout changes when the host dashboard enables the widget.

entries.title: Authentication logs admin index.
entries.notes: Captured in the Laravel 13 demo harness after publishing the package migration and seeding three login audit rows.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews successful and failed authentication events for the demo admin user.

entries.title: Authentication log table filters.
entries.notes: Captured with the table filter panel open on the Login Audit resource.
entries.useCase: An administrator filters authentication events by success state, login date range, or cleared-by-user state.

entries.title: Dashboard widget.
entries.notes: The widget is registered through the Capell dashboard contribution system and is surfaced in dashboard configuration; it does not appear on the default main dashboard layout in the core-only harness.
entries.useCase: A site owner confirms that Access Logs is available in dashboard/widget configuration after the package is installed.

entries.title: Authentication log settings screen.
entries.notes: Captured on the shared Capell settings page after the Login Audit settings schema was registered.
entries.useCase: A site owner configures retention, IP tracking, visibility, and the user-resource bridge for login audit data.

entries.title: User edit access summary.
entries.notes: Captured after adding Rappasoft AuthenticationLoggable to the disposable demo app user model and seeding audit rows.
entries.useCase: An administrator checks recent login counts, failed attempts, devices, and active sessions while editing a user.

entries.title: User authentication logs relation manager.
entries.notes: Requires the host user model to expose authentications(); the default Laravel demo user model needs Rappasoft AuthenticationLoggable for this surface.
entries.useCase: An administrator opens the user-owned authentication history when the host user model supports the authentication log relation.

entries.title: Doctor image action on the Media edit page.
entries.notes: Optional until the runner supports opening resource actions; captures the media surface where the action appears.
entries.useCase: An administrator opens an image media record and uses the Doctor image action when an image doctor provider is bound.

entries.title: Media health page.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/media-library in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews media health status for stale, missing, or incomplete assets.

entries.title: Media health table.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/media-library in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: An administrator scans individual media health rows and remediation state.

entries.title: Curator media field inside a form.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/media-library in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: An editor selects media through the Curator field embedded in a Capell form.

entries.title: Migration command output or report.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/media-library in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: A developer reviews the Spatie-to-Curator migration report before or after migration.

entries.title: Import session index or host admin surface.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/migration-assistant in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews import sessions and their current migration state.

entries.title: Import validation summary.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/migration-assistant in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: An operator reviews validation errors and warnings before executing an import.

entries.title: Recovery page imports.
entries.notes: Show recovery-center page import validation, relation resolution, execution state, and rollback reporting.
entries.useCase: An operator imports pages through Recovery Center and reviews validation, relation resolution, execution, and rollback state.

entries.title: Relation resolution review.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/migration-assistant in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: An operator maps imported references to existing Capell records before execution.

entries.title: Rollback report view.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/migration-assistant in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: An operator reviews what rollback changed and what manual cleanup remains.

entries.title: Package export intent screen.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/migration-assistant in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: An operator prepares an export package and confirms included resources before download.

entries.title: Navigation admin index.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/navigation in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: An editor reviews site navigation records and their scope/status.

entries.title: Create/edit navigation form.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/navigation in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: An editor builds a navigation tree with links, page references, headings, and nested items.

entries.title: Site relation manager for navigations.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/navigation in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: An editor manages navigations directly from the site edit surface.

entries.title: Page form navigation tab.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/navigation in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: An editor controls page-level navigation metadata from the page form.

entries.title: Frontend menu output.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/navigation in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: An anonymous visitor sees the seeded public menu without internal page IDs or editor metadata.

entries.title: Subscribers admin index.
entries.notes: Shows subscriber status, site scope, consent-related columns, filters, and table actions.
entries.useCase: A marketer reviews subscriber status, consent, site scope, tags, and filters.

entries.title: Create/edit subscriber form.
entries.notes: Capture subscriber identity, status, site, tags, and consent-related fields.
entries.useCase: A marketer edits subscriber identity, status, site, tags, and consent-related fields.

entries.title: Provider connections admin index and form.
entries.notes: Use demo-safe provider credentials only.
entries.useCase: An operator configures a newsletter provider connection using demo-safe credentials.

entries.title: Provider audiences admin index and form.
entries.notes: Shows mapping between local sites and remote provider audiences.
entries.useCase: An operator maps local sites to provider audiences.

entries.title: Provider interest mappings admin index and form.
entries.notes: Shows remote interests mapped to local newsletter tags.
entries.useCase: An operator maps remote provider interests to local newsletter tags.

entries.title: Form mappings admin index and form.
entries.notes: Requires form-builder hard dependency and a seeded form.
entries.useCase: A marketer maps a seeded form-builder form into newsletter subscription handling.

entries.title: Newsletter tags admin index and form.
entries.notes: Shows package-owned newsletter tag management.
entries.useCase: A marketer manages package-owned newsletter tags.

entries.title: Segments admin index and form.
entries.notes: Shows static and dynamic segment configuration.
entries.useCase: A marketer configures static or dynamic subscriber segments.

entries.title: Import batches admin index.
entries.notes: Requires seeded import batch state.
entries.useCase: An operator reviews seeded import batch state and import outcomes.

entries.title: Sync attempts admin index.
entries.notes: Shows provider sync state, retry status, and failure handling.
entries.useCase: An operator reviews provider sync attempts, retries, and failures.

entries.title: Newsletter overview stats.
entries.notes: Capture dashboard stats for subscribed, pending, and sync failures.
entries.useCase: A marketer checks subscribed, pending, and sync-failure dashboard stats.

entries.title: Subscription confirmation route response.
entries.notes: Use a disposable valid token and verify no admin/provider internals are exposed.
entries.useCase: A subscriber confirms a disposable newsletter token through the public route.

entries.title: Unsubscribe route response.
entries.notes: Use a disposable valid token and verify no admin/provider internals are exposed.
entries.useCase: A subscriber unsubscribes through a disposable public token without seeing provider internals.

entries.title: Notes inbox page.
entries.notes: Shows the main inbox at /admin/notes.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews notes assigned to them, mentions, and reminders from the inbox.

entries.title: User-menu notes item with attention badge.
entries.notes: Requires seeded assigned, mentioned, or overdue note attention counts.
entries.useCase: An administrator sees the user-menu notes item with an attention badge.

entries.title: Empty notes inbox state.
entries.notes: Shows the default inbox state for a user with no notes requiring attention.
entries.useCase: An administrator sees the default empty inbox state when no notes need attention.

entries.title: Notes inbox with assigned, mentioned, and overdue reminder counts.
entries.notes: Requires seeded notes covering assignment, mention, and reminder states.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews seeded assigned, mentioned, and overdue reminder counts.

entries.title: Password Policy settings page.
entries.notes: Capture the System navigation settings page after the package is installed.
entries.useCase: An administrator configures password age, reuse, complexity, and enforcement rules.

entries.title: Forced password change form.
entries.notes: Capture with a user flagged for password change so the form is visible.
entries.useCase: A flagged administrator is forced through the password-change form before continuing in the admin panel.

entries.title: User table password policy columns and filters.
entries.notes: Capture the core Users table with package-added password status columns, filters, and require-password-change action.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews password status columns, filters users by policy state, and triggers require-password-change actions.

entries.title: Public Actions admin index.
entries.notes: Capture the configured actions list with at least one active action.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews configured public actions and their enabled/site state.

entries.title: Public Action create/edit form.
entries.notes: Show handler, site scope, redirect/messages, API/Zapier toggles, and payload schema fields.
entries.useCase: An administrator configures handler, site scope, redirect/messages, API/Zapier toggles, and payload schema fields.

entries.title: Public Action destinations.
entries.notes: Capture the destination list or form with secrets redacted.
entries.useCase: An administrator configures outbound destinations while secrets stay redacted.

entries.title: Public Action submissions.
entries.notes: Capture received submissions and status after demo data is prepared.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews received submissions and processing status.

entries.title: Public Action dispatch attempts.
entries.notes: Capture outbound dispatch status, adapter, response status, and dispatch time.
entries.useCase: An operator reviews outbound dispatch status, adapter, response status, and dispatch timing.

entries.title: Public Action integration tokens.
entries.notes: Capture the token list and create-token modal without exposing token values.
entries.useCase: An administrator creates or reviews integration tokens without exposing token values.

entries.title: Public Action frontend form.
entries.notes: Capture the public form for an active action with no admin-only metadata visible.
entries.useCase: A visitor submits an active public action form with no admin-only metadata visible.

entries.title: Zapier action discovery API.
entries.notes: Capture or assert the authenticated API response shape using a demo token.
entries.useCase: An integration client verifies the authenticated Zapier action discovery response shape with a demo token.

entries.title: Editorial timeline dashboard
entries.notes: Show the flagship timeline entry point with active drafts, review status, publish readiness, and recent workflow activity. Existing documentation image: docs/images/screenshots/10-workflow-dashboard.png.
entries.useCase: An editor reviews active drafts, review status, publish readiness, and recent workflow activity.

entries.title: Editor responsibility
entries.notes: Show editors managing draft changes while content remains isolated from the live page until a release action runs. Existing documentation image: docs/images/screenshots/11-workspaces-editor-submit.png.
entries.useCase: An editor manages draft changes while live content remains isolated until release.

entries.title: Reviewer responsibility
entries.notes: Show reviewers approving, rejecting, or requesting changes before publish controls become available. Existing documentation image: docs/images/screenshots/12-review-approval.png.
entries.useCase: A reviewer approves, rejects, or requests changes before publish controls become available.

entries.title: Live preview
entries.notes: Show a signed preview URL rendering draft content before it reaches the live version.
entries.useCase: An editor opens a signed preview URL that renders draft content before it reaches the live page.

entries.title: Preview link management
entries.notes: Show issued preview links with expiry, revocation, last accessed time, and access count. Existing documentation image: docs/images/screenshots/13-preview-links.png.
entries.useCase: An editor manages issued preview links, expiry, revocation, last access, and access count.

entries.title: Compare and publish readiness
entries.notes: Show side-by-side changes, field comments, and publish checks before approval or release.
entries.useCase: A reviewer compares field-level changes and publish checks before approving or releasing content.

entries.title: Approval history
entries.notes: Show reviewer assignments and approve, reject, or request-changes decisions in timeline order.
entries.useCase: An editor reviews reviewer assignments and approval decisions in timeline order.

entries.title: Scheduled publishing
entries.notes: Show release timing, scheduled publishing-studio, embargo-aware publishing windows, and queue state. Existing documentation image: docs/images/screenshots/14-scheduled-publishing.png.
entries.useCase: An editor schedules release timing and reviews queue state for future publication.

entries.title: Scheduler metadata
entries.notes: Show unpublish dates, embargo windows, and review reminders attached to a workspace.
entries.useCase: An editor reviews unpublish dates, embargo windows, and review reminders on a workspace.

entries.title: Stale drafts
entries.notes: Show stale workspace detection, review nudges, and cleanup actions for unfinished draft work. Existing documentation image: docs/images/screenshots/15-stale-drafts.png.
entries.useCase: An editor identifies stale workspaces and uses review nudges or cleanup actions.

entries.title: Activity history
entries.notes: Show the audit-friendly content history across previews, comments, approvals, publishing, and rollback events.
entries.useCase: An auditor reviews content history across previews, comments, approvals, publishing, and rollback events.

entries.title: Rollback and restore
entries.notes: Show version history with restore context, rollback lineage, and the currently live version.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews version history, rollback lineage, and the currently live version before restore.

entries.title: Preview banner
entries.notes: Show the frontend workspace banner that confirms editors are viewing draft content and can exit preview mode.
entries.useCase: An editor sees the frontend preview banner confirming draft preview mode and offering an exit path.

entries.title: Frontend search results page.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/search in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: A visitor searches seeded public content and sees ranked results and pagination.

entries.title: Header search field.
entries.notes: Capture with a compatible theme header slot. In a core-only harness, render the package form in a header slot without installing another frontend package.
entries.useCase: A visitor uses the header search control supplied by the package frontend surface.

entries.title: Top searches widget.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/search in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews top searched terms from seeded query logs.

entries.title: Trending searches widget.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/search in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews trending searches over the configured window.

entries.title: Zero-result searches widget.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/search in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: An administrator identifies search terms with no matching content.

entries.title: Site search settings screen.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/search in the isolated demo harness and seeding the data required for this use case.
entries.useCase: A site owner configures search settings, logging, retention, and result behavior.

entries.title: SEO audit page.
entries.notes: Seed at least one page_seo_snapshots row so score, severity, and status columns are visible.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews SEO audit scores and remediation state for seeded pages.

entries.title: Broken links page.
entries.notes: Seed a broken_links row connected to a page so the source page, target URL, status, and redirect action are visible.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews broken link findings and their source pages.

entries.title: Not-found URLs page.
entries.notes: Install and migrate capell-app/insights, then seed at least one page_view event for a missing URL.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews missing frontend URLs grouped by visitor count and latest view.

entries.title: Translation coverage page.
entries.notes: Seed at least one site/page/language combination so translation coverage rows are visible.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews missing or stale translation coverage for SEO-relevant content.

entries.title: AI Discovery page.
entries.notes: Seed AI Discovery site and page profiles so inclusion, summary, readiness, and Markdown columns are visible.
entries.useCase: An administrator manages which public pages appear in AI-readable discovery output.

entries.title: SEO Suite settings page.
entries.notes: Capture the extension settings page after running capell:seo-suite-install so settings rows exist.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews SEO audit, AI Discovery, crawler, Search Console, and AI provider settings.

entries.title: Page SEO panel.
entries.notes: Open a seeded Page edit screen and capture the SEO tab/panel added by SEO Suite.
entries.useCase: An editor edits page-level metadata, canonical state, social metadata, and SEO checks.

entries.title: Search Console insights panel.
entries.notes: Seed search_console_url_metrics so dashboard widgets show clicks, impressions, movement, and opportunities.
entries.useCase: An administrator reviews Search Console-style performance panels when metrics are seeded.

entries.title: llms.txt output.
entries.notes: Seed AI Discovery site/page profiles and use visitor-facing summary text only.
entries.useCase: An AI crawler reads the concise public page index for the site.

entries.title: robots.txt output.
entries.notes: Seed crawler rules or settings and capture the public crawler directives.
entries.useCase: A crawler or operator reviews the public robot directives generated by SEO Suite.

entries.title: Page Markdown output.
entries.notes: Enable Markdown pages on the AI Discovery site profile and capture visitor-safe Markdown.
entries.useCase: An AI crawler reads the Markdown representation of a public page.

entries.title: Page resource sitemap action.
entries.notes: Capture the package-added Sitemap header action on the core Pages resource.
entries.useCase: An editor uses the package-added sitemap action on the core Pages resource.

entries.title: Site resource sitemap action.
entries.notes: Capture the package-added Sitemap header or row action on the core Sites resource.
entries.useCase: An editor uses the package-added sitemap action on the core Sites resource.

entries.title: Sitemap generation tool.
entries.notes: Capture the Livewire sitemap generation tool after demo pages are seeded.
entries.useCase: An administrator generates or reviews sitemap output after demo pages are seeded.

entries.title: Public HTML sitemap page.
entries.notes: Capture public sitemap output with only discoverable public pages visible.
entries.useCase: A visitor opens the public HTML sitemap and sees only discoverable public pages.

entries.title: Generated XML sitemap output.
entries.notes: Run capell:xml-sitemap in the host app before capture. The endpoint serves the generated domain XML file.
entries.useCase: A crawler or operator reviews generated XML sitemap output after running sitemap generation.

entries.title: Tags admin index.
entries.notes: Capture the TagResource index after seeding several tags with different types, status values, and site scopes.
entries.useCase: An editor reviews taxonomy tags and their usage state.

entries.title: Create/edit tag form.
entries.notes: Capture the create form with name, slug, type, site, featured, and status fields visible.
entries.useCase: An editor creates or updates a tag name, slug, locale, and taxonomy metadata.

entries.title: Tag relation manager showing tagged pages.
entries.notes: Seed a taggable Page pivot row, then capture the Pages relation manager on the EditTag page.
entries.useCase: An editor reviews pages or records attached to a tag before changing it.

entries.title: Article or page form using TagsInput.
entries.notes: TagsInput is a reusable component and is only screenshotable when a host package, such as Blog, mounts it in a concrete form.
entries.useCase: An editor assigns tags from a host page or article form using the shared TagsInput field.

entries.title: Themes admin list showing Agency.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/theme-agency with Foundation Theme and creating an Agency theme record in the isolated demo harness.
entries.useCase: An administrator sees the Agency theme record in the host Themes admin surface.

entries.title: Frontend page rendered with Agency theme.
entries.notes: Capture the isolated demo route that renders navigation, hero, features, proof, content listing, CTA, and footer through the Agency renderer.
entries.useCase: A visitor sees a seeded public page rendered through every Agency theme section.

entries.title: Theme preview URL output.
entries.notes: Capture a temporary signed preview URL generated from the host Themes table for the Agency theme.
entries.useCase: An administrator opens a preview URL to inspect a page with the Agency theme applied.

entries.title: Themes admin list showing Corporate.
entries.notes: Capture after installing capell-app/theme-corporate with Foundation Theme and creating a Corporate theme record in the isolated demo harness.
entries.useCase: An administrator sees the Corporate theme record in the host Themes admin surface.

entries.title: Frontend page rendered with Corporate theme.
entries.notes: Capture the isolated demo route that renders navigation, hero, features, proof, content listing, CTA, and footer through the Corporate renderer.
entries.useCase: A visitor sees a seeded public page rendered through every Corporate theme section.

entries.title: Theme preview URL output.
entries.notes: Capture a temporary signed preview URL generated from the host Themes table for the Corporate theme.
entries.useCase: An administrator opens a preview URL to inspect a page with the Corporate theme applied.

entries.title: Theme admin list showing SaaS.
entries.notes: Capture with only the core Capell stack, Foundation Theme, Layout Builder, and capell-app/theme-saas installed in the isolated demo harness.
entries.useCase: An administrator confirms the SaaS theme is available through the core Themes resource without installing another theme package.

entries.title: Frontend page rendered with SaaS theme.
entries.notes: Capture the seeded Launchdeck page after installing capell-app/theme-saas and building the Foundation Theme/frontend assets.
entries.useCase: A visitor sees a seeded product page rendered through the SaaS navigation, hero, features, proof, content listing, CTA, and footer sections.

entries.title: Theme preview URL output.
entries.notes: Capture a signed preview URL generated with CreateThemePreviewUrlAction for the seeded SaaS theme, site, and page.
entries.useCase: An administrator opens a signed preview URL to inspect a page with the SaaS theme applied before publishing the theme choice.

entries.title: Translation Manager page with source, locale, file, and filter selectors in the no-results state.
entries.notes: Capture after installing only the core Capell stack and capell-app/translation-manager, with package_paths restricted to [] in the disposable harness so unrelated local package repositories do not appear as sources.
entries.useCase: An administrator filters the current translation file to a state with no matching rows and still sees the source, locale, file, and filter controls.

entries.title: Translation comparison grid with source strings, editable target strings, statuses, and entry selection.
entries.notes: Seed the application source with an en file and a partial fr file so the grid shows missing and changed entries without relying on another package.
entries.useCase: A translator reviews source strings beside editable target strings, status, and selection controls.

entries.title: Create locale modal.
entries.notes: Open the Create locale header action from /admin/translation-manager.
entries.useCase: A translator opens the Create locale action to add a new target locale.

entries.title: Duplicate locale modal.
entries.notes: Open the Duplicate locale header action from /admin/translation-manager after en and fr locales exist.
entries.useCase: A translator opens the Duplicate locale action to copy strings from an existing locale.

entries.title: Translate selected action visible with AI translator support.
entries.notes: Requires an available TranslationAITranslator binding, typically through the optional AI Orchestrator integration.
entries.useCase: A translator runs or reviews the Translate selected action when an AI translator binding is available.

entries.title: Admin dashboard rendered through WelcomeTourDashboard.
entries.notes: Capture after installing only the core Capell stack and capell-app/welcome-tour, with capell-app/login-audit and its direct authentication-log dependency removed from the disposable harness.
entries.useCase: A first-time administrator lands on the dashboard replacement that can launch onboarding.

entries.title: Welcome tour overlay showing the first configured onboarding step.
entries.notes: Use an admin account that has not dismissed the tour.
entries.useCase: A first-time administrator sees the first configured tour step overlay.

entries.title: Welcome tour settings group with enabled toggle and step repeater.
entries.notes: Open the Welcome Tour package settings page after publishing and running the package settings migration.
entries.useCase: A site owner configures the enabled state and tour step repeater from the package settings page.

entries.title: User edit form with Show welcome tour toggle.
entries.notes: Requires a users table with the dismissed_hints column so the bridge contributes the field.
entries.useCase: An administrator toggles whether a user should see the welcome tour again from the user edit form.

entries.title: Migration Assistant import flow with WordPress WXR available as an import source.
entries.notes: Install Migration Assistant first, then WordPress Importer. Capture the source selection state where the WXR reader is visible.
entries.useCase: An operator sees WordPress WXR as an available source in the Migration Assistant import flow.

entries.title: Parsed WordPress WXR rows previewed in Migration Assistant.
entries.notes: Use a small WXR fixture with at least one post and one page to show extracted title, slug, status, date, categories, tags, and author fields.
entries.useCase: An operator previews parsed WXR posts/pages with title, slug, status, date, categories, tags, and author fields.

entries.title: Import session detail for a WordPress WXR import.
entries.notes: Migration Assistant owns the page; include this screenshot when a demo import session can be created.
entries.useCase: An operator reviews the Migration Assistant import session created for a WordPress WXR import.

assertions: no edit_url, recordKey, model, field path, selector, label, or signed URL in the response or DOM
beacon returns an authoring bootstrap script
page title edit control appears after the beacon runs
clicking the control opens a same-origin signed iframe
iframe renders exactly one Filament field
